MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Selwyn D. Whitehead; Law Offices of Selwyn D. Whitehead; 510-632-7444; appearing via Zoom on behalf of defendants Dwight W. Davenport and BJT Holdings.
Alan P. Jacobus; Shannon B. Jones Law Group, Inc.; 925-837-2317; appearing via Zoom on behalf of defendants Elk Hall Investors, Inc dba Keller Williams Realty Elk Grove and Yolanda Faye James-Elston.
Christopher J. Waldon; Venable LLP; 415-653-3750; appearing via Zoom on behalf of Plaintiff Virgil Denise Bradley-Tyson.

Dwight W. Davenport; defendant, in attendance via Zoom.
Virgil Denise Bradley-Tyson; plaintiff, in attendance via Zoom.

Hearing held.

Virgil Denise Bradley-Tyson is placed under oath and testifies that she and her late husband Bernard Tyson purchased a condo at 301 Main St #376 in San Francisco and that title would be held jointly in both their names. The property was discussed with Dwight Davenport prior to purchase. Mr. Davenport was her late husband's friend and tax preparer. She considered Mr. Davenport as advisor to both Mr. Tyson and herself regarding the purchase of the property. Prior to the purchase of the property, she also spoke with Mr. Davenport while not in the presence of Mr. Tyson.

Dwight W. Davinport is placed under oath and testifies that he grew up with Mr. Bernard Tyson, and that they have been friends since around 1965, when they were 5 years old. Mr. Tyson's father was a client of his, and he began preparing taxes for Mr. Tyson as soon as began working. He did not know Mrs. Bradley-Tyson proir to her marriage with Mr. Tyson. He did not have a contract with Mrs. Bradley-Tyson or Mr. Tyson. Mr. Tyson filed his taxes individually, as head of household, and claimed only his two sons as dependents. Mrs. Bradley Tyson was not listed on any of Mr. Tyson's tax forms, and taxes were never discussed with Mrs. Bradley-Tyson. Mr. Tyson would meet with Mr. Davenport privately to discuss taxes, as he did not want to include his wife in the discussions. Mr. Tyson and Mrs. Bradley-Tyson purchased the condo on 5/3/2018. Mrs. Bradley Tyson quitclaimed on 5/21/2018. Title was transferred to BJT on 1/29/2019, with 25% interest to Mrs. Bradley-Tyson and 75% to BJT Holdings. The City of San Francisco notified the parties on 8/8/2019 that there was a problem with the deed. Mr. Tyson took no action and passed away on 11/10/2019. BJT Holdings was formed on 6/7/2018. He prepared taxes for BJT, and is a 50% shareholder with Mr. Tyson.

After reviewing the pleadings, listening to oral arguments, and hearing live witness testimony, the Court rules as follows:

DEFENDANTS DWIGHT DAVENPORT AND BJT HOLDINGS, INC.'S NOTICE OF AND MOTION FOR SANCTIONS PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 128.7 is taken under submission.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Courtcall:
Christopher J.C Waldon, Esq., (415) 653-3750 , appearing for Virgil Denise Bradley Tyson, Plaintiff
Alan P. Jacobus, Esq.,(925) 837-2317, appearing for Elk Hall Investors, Inc. et.
al., for Defendant
Selwyn D. Whitehead, Esq., (510) 632-7444, appearing for Dwight Davenport, Defendant

ARGUED. Mr. Waldon stated that he contested the tentative ruling, argued that he should not have to amend anything and proivded case backgropund. Mr. Jacobus presented oral argument in support of his position.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DWIGHT DAVENPORT, BJT HOLDINGS, INC. DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to fraud-based causes of action for Plaintiff to allege reliance of Davenport's representations (as opposed to reliance on decedent's representations due to the nature of this marital relationship). Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend for causes of action for quiet title, reformation and unjust enrichment as to Davenport individually. Demurrer is overruled at to these causes of action as to BJT Holdings, Inc to the extent Plaintiff is able to allege fraud or these causes of action are based solely on mistake (if legally proper).
Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)
